front: Compare Apache Iceberg, Delta Lake, and Apache Hudi — what are the key differences?
back: All three are open table formats for data lakehouses providing ACID transactions, schema evolution, and time travel on object storage. Delta Lake (Databricks-originated, open-sourced 2019): deeply integrated with Spark, uses a transaction log (_delta_log directory of JSON/Parquet files), excellent Databricks tooling (auto-optimize, liquid clustering). Strongest for Spark-only shops on Databricks. Iceberg (Netflix-originated, Apache 2018): engine-agnostic (Spark, Flink, Trino, Presto, Snowflake all support it natively), richest partitioning capabilities (partition evolution, hidden partitioning), REST catalog becoming universal standard. Best for multi-engine architectures. Apache Hudi (Uber-originated): specializes in streaming upserts and incremental processing, native MoR support, built-in incremental query API for downstream consumers. Best for CDC-heavy pipelines with frequent updates. Market trajectory: Iceberg is winning as the multi-engine standard (AWS, Azure, Snowflake, Google all adopted it). Delta Lake dominates Databricks. Hudi still used at Uber, Amazon internally but losing mindshare. For new greenfield: Iceberg unless you're Databricks-only.
